plain_english_summary: Deterministic sensor compression SDK for IoT time-series and chemosense streams.
commercial_wedge: Deterministic replay and bounded-lossy fidelity guarantees for industrial IoT sensor streams. 6.65× mean compression on real public datasets with 9 domain-aware presets.
technical_wedge: Rust-core eight-direction sensor codec with Python bindings. 6.65× mean CR (DS-01..DS-10), 27/27 destructive-test pass, decode-deterministic replay.
ideal_first_buyer: Industrial IoT engineering team or edge telemetry vendor
deployment_model: PyPI package is live. Rust core and Python bindings remain repo-local engineering surfaces. Public posture is always-in-beta: useful now, improving continuously.
not_claimed: Broader release ceremony beyond the current PyPI package and cited proof surface
Universal compressor dominance (DS-12 competitor win, DS-11 blocked)
Direct Gorilla parity (Gorilla-proxy comparator is a simplified XOR+zlib proxy, not Facebook's production codec)
Unlimited stream length (65,536-sample hard cap via 2-byte header; ~16 min at 60 Hz)
NaN/Inf tolerance (non-finite inputs cause codec failure)
Signal-level roundtrip idempotency (encode(decode(encode(x))) ≠ encode(x) is expected for bounded-lossy codec; determinism applies to decode path only)

benchmark_methodology.cr_note: Against float32 baselines, ratios are approximately half
benchmark_methodology.gorilla_proxy: Simplified ~25-line XOR-delta + zlib proxy inspired by Facebook Gorilla; not the production codec
benchmark_methodology.lossy_vs_lossless: ZPE-IoT is bounded-lossy; baselines (zstd, LZ4, zlib, Gorilla-proxy) are lossless

family_position: Independent encoding product in the Zer0pa portfolio. IMC alignment is documentary and contractual, not a runtime dependency.
